title: Banoffee tart – the richest of all rich treats
excerpt: Sometimes people cross your path to stay. When I'd just started the blog, Rista Greyvenstein asked me for a post toastie tart recipe. I'll never forget it. Now the other day she asked me for a banoffee tart and woke up flavours in me I'd already forgotten about …

seo.title: Banoffee tart - the richest of all rich treats - Marinda Kook

html:
Sometimes people cross your path to stay. When I'd just started the blog, Rista Greyvenstein asked me for a post toastie tart recipe. I'll never forget it. Now the other day she asked me for a benoffee tart and woke up flavours in me that I'd already forgotten. Rista, here it is. I'm going to give a fussy way and an easy way. I used the longer road – it tastes better!

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 1 packet biscuits – tennis, romany creams, digestive (whichever you like)
  • 125 ml melted butter
  • 3 bananas
  • 250 ml cream
  • 60 ml castor sugar
  • 1 tin condensed milk
  • 1 tin caramel treat
  • 1/3 (70g) brown sugar – that kind that looks like it's alive
  • 50 g butter
  • 1 flake

NOW:

  1. chop the biscuits fine in your food processor
  2. pour in the 125 ml melted butter
  3. spray a loose-bottomed pan and press the crust firmly into it – put it in the fridge for at least 30 minutes
  4. pour the condensed milk (not the caramel treat), 50 g butter and brown sugar into a pot
  5. now you must stir continuously for about 10 minutes – until it thickens
  6. take it off the stove and stir in the caramel treat
  7. pour it over your crust and put it in the fridge for 30 minutes
  8. slice the bananas into rounds and arrange on the caramel layer
  9. whip the cream and castor sugar and spread over the bananas
  10. crumble the flake and scatter over the cream layer
  11. let it rest for at least 1 hour in the fridge

This tart is an old favourite and deeply delicious. When I eat it I can feel goosebumps rising on my cheeks.

For a quick benoffee tart you can skip cooking the condensed milk and just whip up 2 tins of caramel treat and put it on your base. I do feel the long way makes it creamier, though.

Be warned – it's addictive!
